chunks.section: Boys Hostel & Girls Hostel — Detailed Descriptions
chunks.context: This chunk provides detailed prose descriptions of both the Boys Hostel and Girls Hostel at MSAJCE, covering amenities, atmosphere, library access timings, and connectivity features.
chunks.text: Boys Hostel: A home away from home is provided for boys, accommodating 480 boy students. All rooms are well furnished with modern amenities such as cot, mattress with pillows, bed-spreads, individual cup-boards, chair-table with lamp and wall hangers. Each room accommodates only 2 persons. A hygienic canteen is available for dining within the premises. An entertainment hall with LCD-TV, indoor game facilities, reading room with newspapers and popular magazines are available. The Hostel has land-line telephone and Wi-Fi internet access. The College Main Library and the Computer Centre are kept open till 7:00 PM for the benefit of hostellers. Students will feel more at home and concentrate on their studies, facilitated by a serene atmosphere filled with greenery. Girls Hostel: The MSAJCE Girls Hostel is situated at Sholinganallur, 5 KM away from the campus. This hostel is located in a main posh area with safety and security. There are about 71 rooms, accommodating 3 girl students per room. All rooms are furnished with cot, mattress with pillows, bed-spreads, individual cup-boards, chair-table with lamp, and wall hangers. Each room is provided with bath and toilet facilities, wash basin and mirror. Entertainment hall with LCD-TV, indoor games, reading room are available. Library and Computer facility are open till 9:00 PM.

chunks.section: Hostel Rules & Dining Hall — Mess Timings
chunks.context: This chunk covers the 8 official hostel rules at MSAJCE, dining hall description, and the complete mess timings table for both working days and holidays.
chunks.text: Hostel Rules: 1. Students must occupy the rooms allotted to them by the Warden (Principal). 2. Students should refrain from anti-social and undesirable activities such as consumption of alcohol, tobacco, gambling, ragging etc. 3. Students are responsible for the cleanliness of their rooms. 4. No student will be allowed to leave the hostel based on a phone call. 5. Parents are requested to give a list of authorized visitors or local guardians. Visitors not mentioned in the list will not be permitted to visit the students in the hostel. 6. Students will be permitted to go home only if the college is closed continuously for five days or more. 7. Any student wishing to attend a function in the house, marriage or any other ceremony will be permitted to go only if the request is made by the parents to the Principal. 8. Visitors are allowed on holidays from 11:00 A.M. to 6:00 P.M. Dining Hall: Hostel Mess is run by the Management through a private contractor under the guidance of the Principal and Administrative Officer. Both Vegetarian and Non-Vegetarian food is served. Mess charges are collected on an annual basis at the time of admission. Parents and guests are allowed to consume food by paying for them. Separate seating arrangements are made for both boys and girls. Mess Timings — Breakfast (Working Days): 07:00 AM to 08:00 AM; (Holidays): 07:30 AM to 09:00 AM. Lunch (Working Days): 01:00 PM to 01:45 PM; (Holidays): 12:30 PM to 02:00 PM. Dinner (Working Days): 07:00 PM to 08:30 PM; (Holidays): 07:00 PM to 09:00 PM.

chunks.section: Games & TV Timings — Study Hours & Cafeteria Details
chunks.context: This chunk covers the scheduled timings for games, TV, and study hours in the MSAJCE hostel, along with complete details about the college cafeteria including capacity, timings, and food served.
chunks.text: Games and TV Timings: On working days, games and TV are permitted from 04:30 PM to 06:00 PM. On holidays, games and TV are allowed from 09:00 AM to 12:00 PM and again from 01:00 PM to 06:00 PM. Study Hours — Morning: 05:00 AM to 07:00 AM. Study Hours — Evening: 06:00 PM to 07:00 PM and 09:00 PM to 10:30 PM. Cafeteria: MSAJCE has a spacious and hygienic cafeteria that caters to the taste of all students. It can conveniently accommodate 100 students at a time. The cafeteria is provided with modern furniture, electrical cooking and storing facilities which provides delicious breakfast, lunch, snacks and beverages both for students and staff at reasonable rates. Separate space is available for students and staff. It is well maintained with effective service. The cafeteria remains open on all working days from 8:00 AM to 8:00 PM.

chunks.section: Sathak Innovation and Incubation Foundation (SIIF) — Overview & Incubation Cell
chunks.context: Describes the founding objects of SIIF and the services offered by the Incubation Cell including infrastructure, funding, mentorship, and events.
chunks.text: Sathak Innovation and Incubation Foundation (SIIF) — Main Objects: a) To promote commerce, science, education and research by helping, establishing and incubating startups, individuals, teams with innovative ideas to make prototypes and convert them into products. b) To promote a culture of innovation-driven entrepreneurship in the fields of science, engineering, technology, commerce, agriculture, health care, medicine, pharmacy, education, arts, sports and other various fields. c) To establish, help establish and/or manage infrastructure, laboratories, to make and test prototypes/products, pilot plant production facilities, software development facilities, training and human resource development centres, propagate new scientific and technological development and innovation from idea stage to full-scale commercial production or any part of this chain. For the above purposes to assist, co-operate or collaborate with any establishment, laboratory, technology business incubators centre, organisation or institution in India or abroad. Incubation Cell — Services Offered: Physical office space: approx. 5000 sq.ft. Event halls: 100-seater, 400-seater, 700-seater halls for Entrepreneurship events. Funding opportunities: grants, seed funding, and connections to potential investors. Mentorship programs: experienced professionals and industry experts provide guidance. Access to network of investors, venture capitalists, and angel investors. Workshops, training programs, and events for innovation and entrepreneurial skills. Partnerships with academic institutions, industry bodies, and government agencies. Incubation Lead: Name Role Organisation Email Website Akshita K Incubation Lead SIIF, MSAJCE — Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering, Chennai - 603103 info@siif.ventures www.siif.ventures

chunks.section: National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) — Vision, Mission & Objectives
chunks.context: Covers the NISP vision and mission statements and both short-term and long-term objectives for promoting innovation and entrepreneurship at MSAJCE.
chunks.text: National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) — MSAJCE Vision: To cater the needs of student entrepreneurs with innovative ideas thereby introducing a culture of entrepreneurship inside campus which will strengthen our education system and thereby promoting national economic and social growth. Mission: To develop an ecosystem with required infrastructure that can enable students and faculty to innovate and prototype their potential ideas with industrial standards and support from Government, industry and reputed academic institutions around the world and help them to realize their potentials. Short Term Objectives: 1. To help student groups to prototype their ideas. 2. To improve innovation, creative and design thinking among student community. 3. Incubation facility for faculty driven start-up and student/alumni start-up. 4. Organize FDP, seminars and workshops, distinguish talks for students, faculty and alumni and promote entrepreneurial culture. 5. Strengthen institute-industry interaction cell activity and effectively use the outcomes for achieving the mission. Long Term Objectives: 1. Associate with DST, CII, MSME and other academic institutions for transferring world class facility for MSAJCE IEDC activities. 2. Improve quality of research work among students and to attain patent, which can be commercially used in production. 3. Provide a platform for students to develop innovative products with global recognition and generate business opportunities. 4. Generate revenues through consultancy work and student start-ups. 5. Spread awareness to students and faculty regarding IPR related activities. 6. Strategic partnership linkage with: a. Entrepreneurship Development Institute of India (EDII), Chennai. b. National Innovation Foundation (NIF) of India to submit ideas and apply for schemes. c. Procure fund from AICTE for Entrepreneurship Development Cell.

chunks.section: Institution Innovation Council (IIC) — About, Vision, Mission & Office Bearers
chunks.context: Covers the IIC constitution, its major functions, vision, mission, and office bearer details for MSAJCE's Institution Innovation Council.
chunks.text: Institution Innovation Council (IIC) — MSAJCE IIC-MSAJCE has been constituted as per guidelines of the Ministry of Human Resource Development Innovation Cell (MIC). Introduction: Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD), Govt. of India has established 'MHRD's Innovation Cell (MIC)' to systematically foster the culture of innovation amongst all Higher Education Institutions (HEIs). The primary mandate of MIC is to encourage, inspire and nurture young students by supporting them to work with new ideas and transform them into prototypes while they are in their formative years. MIC has envisioned encouraging creation of 'Institution's Innovation Councils (IICs)' across selected HEIs. A network of IICs is established to promote innovation in institutions through multitudinous modes leading to an innovation promotion eco-system in campuses. Major Focus of IIC: To create a vibrant local innovation ecosystem. Start-up supporting mechanism in HEIs. Prepare institute for Atal Ranking of Institutions on Innovation Achievements (ARIIA) Framework. Establish Function at Ecosystem for Scouting Ideas and Pre-incubation of Ideas. Develop better Cognitive Ability for Technology Students. Functions of IIC: Identify and reward innovations and share success stories. Organize periodic workshops/seminars/interactions with entrepreneurs, investors, professionals and create a mentor pool for student innovators. Network with peers and national entrepreneurship development organizations. Create an Institution's Innovation Portal to highlight innovative projects by institution's faculty and students. Organize Hackathons, idea competitions, mini-challenges with industry involvement. IIC Vision: To be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient Electrical and Electronics Engineers through sustainable practices. IIC Mission: M1. Impart core fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in Electrical and Electronics Engineering through innovative teaching and learning methodology. M2. Inculcate critical thinking, ethics, lifelong learning and creativity needed for industry and society. M3. Cultivate students with all-round competencies for career, higher education and self-employability. IIC Office Bearers: Role Name Designation President Mr. S. Vimalathithan Associate Professor & NISP Coordinator Convenor Dr. Ramya — Student-Convenor Mr. Yogesh R B.Tech - IT (2022–2026) IIC Contact: Head of Institution: Dr. K.S. Srinivasan — Principal, MSAJCE, Chennai - 603103 Email: principal@msajce-edu.in IIC President: Mr. S. Vimalathithan — Associate Professor, IIC President, MSAJCE, Chennai - 603103 Email: iic.head@msajce-edu.in

chunks.section: IQAC — Overview, Purpose and Background
chunks.context: Explains what IQAC is, why it was established per NAAC guidelines, and its overall role in sustaining quality in MSAJCE — critical for accreditation and institutional queries.
chunks.text: INTERNAL QUALITY ASSURANCE CELL (IQAC) — Overview In pursuance of the National Action Plan of the National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC), Bangalore, for performance evaluation, assessment and accreditation and quality upgradation of institutions of higher education, the NAAC proposes that every accredited institution should establish an Internal Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) as a post-accreditation quality sustenance measure. Since quality enhancement is a continuous process, the IQAC will become a part of an institution's system and work towards realizing the goals of quality enhancement and sustenance. The prime task of the IQAC is to develop a system for conscious, consistent and catalytic improvement in the performance of institutions. The IQAC will make a significant and meaningful contribution in the post-accreditation phase of institutions. During the post-accreditation period, the IQAC will channelise the efforts and measures of an institution towards academic excellence. The guidelines provided will facilitate the institution in the creation and operation of the IQAC. The work of the IQAC is the first step towards the internalization and institutionalization of quality enhancement. Its success depends upon the sense of belongingness and participation it can inculcate in all the constituents of the institution. It will not be yet another hierarchical structure or record-keeping exercise in the institution; it will be a facilitative and participative voluntary system of the institution. The IQAC has the potential to become a vehicle for ushering in quality by working out intervention strategies to remove deficiencies and enhance quality. Quality circles in industries operate on similar lines. IQAC Page URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/iqac.php NAAC Page: https://www.msajce-edu.in/naac.php

chunks.section: IQAC — Objectives, Strategies and Functions
chunks.context: Details the specific objectives, strategies and functions of the IQAC at MSAJCE — important for understanding how quality is managed institutionally.
chunks.text: IQAC Objectives: To develop a system for conscious, consistent and catalytic action to improve the academic and administrative performance of the institution. To promote measures for the institutional functioning towards quality enhancement through internalization of quality culture and institutionalization of best practices. IQAC Strategies — Mechanisms and Procedures for: Ensuring timely, efficient and progressive performance of academic, administrative and financial tasks. The relevance and quality of academic and research programmes. Equitable access to and affordability of academic programmes for various sections of society. Optimization and integration of modern methods of teaching and learning. The credibility of evaluation procedures. Ensuring the adequacy, maintenance and functioning of the support structure and services. Research sharing and networking with other institutions in India and abroad. IQAC Functions: Development and application of quality benchmarks/parameters for the various academic and administrative activities of the institution. Dissemination of information on the various quality parameters of higher education. Organization of workshops, seminars on quality related themes and promotion of quality circles. Documentation of the various programmes and activities leading to quality improvement. Acting as a nodal agency of the institution for quality-related activities. Preparation of the Annual Quality Assurance Report (AQAR) to be submitted to NAAC based on the quality parameters.

chunks.section: IQAC — Benefits, Coordinator Role and Operational Features
chunks.context: Describes the benefits of IQAC, the crucial role of the IQAC Coordinator/Director, and the operational features that guide quality assurance at MSAJCE.
chunks.text: IQAC Benefits — IQAC will facilitate and contribute: To a heightened level of clarity and focus in institutional functioning towards quality enhancement and facilitate internalization of the quality culture. To the enhancement and integration among the various activities of the institution and institutionalize many good practices. To provide a sound basis for decision making to improve institutional functioning. To act as a change agent in the institution. To better internal communication. The Role of Coordinator / Director: The role of the coordinator of the IQAC is crucial in ensuring the effective functioning of all the members. The coordinator of the IQAC may be a senior person with expertise in quality aspects. She/he may be a full-time functionary or, to start with, she/he may be a senior academic/administrator entrusted with the IQAC as an additional responsibility. Secretarial assistance may be facilitated by the administration. It is preferable that the coordinator may have a sound knowledge about the computer, its various functions and usage for effective communication. Operational Features of the IQAC: Quality assurance is a by-product of ongoing efforts to define the objectives of an institution, to have a work plan to achieve them and to specify the checks and balances to evaluate the degree to which each of the tasks is fulfilled. Hence devotion and commitment to improvement rather than mere institutional control is the basis for devising procedures and instruments for assuring quality. The right balance between the health and growth of an institution needs to be struck. The IQAC has to ensure that whatever is done in the institution for education is done efficiently and effectively with high standards. In order to do this, the IQAC will have to first establish procedures and modalities to collect data and information on various aspects of institutional functioning. The coordinators of the IQAC and the secretary have a major role in implementing these functions.

To provide a sound basis for decision making to improve institutional functioning. To act as a change agent in the institution. To better internal communication. The Role of Coordinator / Director: The role of the coordinator of the IQAC is crucial in ensuring the effective functioning of all the members. The coordinator of the IQAC may be a senior person with expertise in quality aspects. She/he may be a full-time functionary or, to start with, she/he may be a senior academic/administrator entrusted with the IQAC as an additional responsibility. Secretarial assistance may be facilitated by the administration. It is preferable that the coordinator may have a sound knowledge about the computer, its various functions and usage for effective communication. Operational Features of the IQAC: Quality assurance is a by-product of ongoing efforts to define the objectives of an institution, to have a work plan to achieve them and to specify the checks and balances to evaluate the degree to which each of the tasks is fulfilled. Hence devotion and commitment to improvement rather than mere institutional control is the basis for devising procedures and instruments for assuring quality. The right balance between the health and growth of an institution needs to be struck. The IQAC has to ensure that whatever is done in the institution for education is done efficiently and effectively with high standards. In order to do this, the IQAC will have to first establish procedures and modalities to collect data and information on various aspects of institutional functioning. The coordinators of the IQAC and the secretary have a major role in implementing these functions.
KEYWORDS: IQAC benefits, IQAC coordinator, IQAC director, change agent, quality assurance, operational features, internal communication, decision making, checks and balances, institutional functioning, good practices, quality culture, work plan

chunks.section: Department Overview and B.Tech IT Programme Details
chunks.context: Overview of the Information Technology department at MSAJCE and full details of the B.Tech IT programme including duration, seats, eligibility, and curriculum links.
chunks.text: Department of Information Technology — MSAJCE The role of Information Technology is vital in every aspect of human life. The information technology is the study and use of systems for storing, retrieving and processing information. With the introduction of computers, the business world has changed forever; using computers ensure smooth running of the business. Information technology helps the business world to view changes in the global markets faster than they usually do. Due to the development of this technology, globalization has increased and the world is brought closer. Information is stored and shared quickly and easily across the globe. Communication has become easier and cheaper. Using internet, Skype, people can speak to each other all over the globe. Social media and mobile devices make the people closer to each other. The key fields include Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Cloud Computing, Networking, Mobile computing, Block Chain and so on, which have tremendous job openings. Programme: B.Tech. — Information Technology Duration: 4 years (Regular) / 3 years (Lateral Entry) No. of Semesters: 8 (Regular) / 6 (Lateral Entry) Intake / No. of Seats: Total — 60 (Government — 30, Management — 30) Eligibility: 10+2 system of Education. Must have secured a pass in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics in the qualifying examination. Curriculum & Syllabus Links: 2021 Regulation: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/academics/2021Regulation.pdf 2017 Regulation: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/academics/2017Regulation.pdf Career Opportunities: Planners, Designers, Constructors & Operators Stewards of the Environment Innovators & Integrators of Technology Managers of Risk Leaders in Public Policy Public Sector Undertaking Government Sector Scope for Higher Studies: M.E. / M.Tech. / M.B.A.

chunks.context: The IT department's vision, mission statements, Programme Educational Objectives and Programme Specific Outcomes for the B.Tech IT programme.
chunks.text: Vision: To be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient IT professionals through sustainable practices. Mission: M1. Impart core fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in Information Technology through innovative teaching and learning methodology M2. Inculcate critical thinking, ethics, lifelong learning and creativity needed for industry and society M3. Cultivate the students with all-round competencies, for career, higher education and self-employability Programme Educational Objectives (PEOs): PEO1 PEO Description PEO1 Graduates will be prepared for designing and developing efficient Information Technology systems and software with creativity and sustainability PEO2 Graduates will be skilled in the use of modern tools for critical problem solving and analyzing industrial and societal requirements PEO3 Graduates will be prepared with managerial and leadership skills for career and starting up own firms Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs): Engineering Graduates will be able to: PSO1 Apply the acquired knowledge and adapt modern tools to provide the innovative solutions in Networking and Cyber Security applications PSO2 Design, implement and test mobile apps and software solutions for various automation applications

chunks.section: Programme Outcomes (POs)
chunks.context: The 12 Programme Outcomes (POs) for the B.Tech IT programme at MSAJCE defining graduate competencies.
chunks.text: Programme Outcomes (PO) — B.Tech Information Technology, MSAJCE PO1 — Engineering Knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems. PO2 — Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences. PO3 — Design/Development Of Solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for the public health and safety, and the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. PO4 — Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions. PO5 — Modern Tool Usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities with an understanding of the limitations. PO6 — The Engineer and Society: Apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to the professional engineering practice. PO7 — Environment and Sustainability: Understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development. PO8 — Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice. PO9 — Individual and Team Work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multi disciplinary settings. PO10 — Communication: Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large. PO11 — Project Management and Finance: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the engineering and management principles and apply these to one's own work. PO12 — Life-long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change.

chunks.section: Job Profiles and IT Employment Forecast 2025–2030
chunks.context: IT job opportunities in India and abroad, employment forecast for 2025-2030, and key drivers of IT industry growth.
chunks.text: Job Profiles — IT Department, MSAJCE Job opportunities are available in various MNCs in service and product companies as System Engineers, Software Professionals, Network Engineers, System Analysts, System Administrators, Project Leaders, Education sector, System Architects and Academicians. IT Employment Forecast in India — 2025 to 2030: The Information Technology (IT) industry in India is poised for strong growth over the next five years, driven by domestic and global trends. Key Drivers of IT Growth: 1. Digital Transformation: Businesses across all sectors are adopting Cloud computing, AI, Big Data, IoT, and Blockchain. Demand for cloud engineers, data scientists, AI specialists, and DevOps engineers will rise. 2. Cloud Computing: Cloud market in India is growing rapidly. AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud will create high demand for cloud architects, cloud administrators, and cloud security specialists. Hybrid cloud solutions will generate opportunities in cloud migration, cloud operations, and cloud infrastructure management. 3. Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning: AI is transforming healthcare, finance, manufacturing, and retail. By 2030, demand will rise for data scientists, AI researchers, machine learning engineers, and AI-enabled software developers. Skills needed: AI ethics, deep learning, AI model deployment. 4. Cyber Security: With increasing cyberattacks and regulations (GDPR, Personal Data Protection Bill), demand for cybersecurity experts will grow. Roles: ethical hackers, incident response professionals, security architects, SOC analysts. 5. Fintech and Blockchain: India's cashless economy push requires blockchain developers, smart contract auditors, and DeFi specialists. 6. 5G and Telecommunications: Rollout of 5G will create demand for 5G network engineers, telecommunications architects, and telecom software developers. IoT will see a boom in smart cities, agriculture, and healthcare. 7. E-Governance and Smart Cities: Government initiatives like Digital India, Smart Cities Mission, and Make in India will create roles for IT project managers, data engineers, IoT developers, and cloud solutions architects. 8. Outsourcing and Off-shoring: India will continue as a global IT outsourcing hub with demand for application developers, IT consultants, systems integrators, and support specialists.

chunks.section: IT Domain Employment Opportunities and Salary Expectations
chunks.context: Detailed breakdown of IT domain-wise employment opportunities with job roles, skills, certifications, and salary expectations in India for 2025-2030.
chunks.text: Employment Opportunities in IT Domain (2025–2030): 1. Cloud Computing and Virtualization: Job Roles: Cloud Engineer, Cloud Solutions Architect, Cloud Security Specialist, DevOps Engineer, Cloud Administrator Skills: AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Cloud security (IAM, encryption), Containers (Docker, Kubernetes), Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Ansible) Certifications: AWS Certified Solutions Architect, Microsoft Certified Azure Solutions Architect Expert, Google Cloud Professional Cloud Architect, Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) 2. Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning: Job Roles: Data Scientist, AI Engineer, Machine Learning Engineer, NLP Specialist, Deep Learning Engineer Skills: Python, R, Java; ML frameworks (TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch); Data visualization (Matplotlib, Seaborn); AI/ML algorithms and statistical models Certifications: Microsoft Certified Azure AI Engineer Associate, Google Professional Machine Learning Engineer, IBM AI Engineering Professional Certificate, AWS Certified Machine Learning – Specialty 3. Cyber Security: Job Roles: Security Analyst, Ethical Hacker (Penetration Tester), Security Architect, SOC Analyst, Cybersecurity Consultant Skills: Risk management, Ethical hacking tools (Metasploit, Burp Suite), Firewalls, SIEM tools (Splunk, LogRhythm), Incident response and forensics Certifications: CEH, CISSP, CISM, CompTIA Security+, CISA 4. Blockchain and Fintech: Job Roles: Blockchain Developer, Smart Contract Developer, Blockchain Architect, Cryptocurrency Expert, Blockchain Security Engineer Skills: Blockchain platforms (Ethereum, Hyperledger, Solana), Smart contract development (Solidity, Rust), DApp development Certifications: Certified Blockchain Developer, Ethereum Developer Certification, Certified Blockchain Professional (CBCP) 5. IT Infrastructure and Networking: Job Roles: Network Administrator, IT Systems Engineer, IT Infrastructure Manager, Virtualization Engineer, Network Security Engineer Skills: TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP, VMware, Hyper-V, VPN, IDS/IPS, firewalls Certifications: CCNA, CompTIA Network+, CISA, VMware Certified Professional (VCP) Expected Employment Growth Trends: Annual Growth Rate: Indian IT industry expected to grow at 7-10% per annum, especially in Cloud computing, Cyber security, AI, and data science. Job Shift: Towards specialized IT roles — Cloud engineers, AI specialists, Cyber security experts, Data analysts. Salary Growth: Cyber security, AI, data science, and Blockchain expected to see highest growth with 20-30% higher salaries compared to other IT positions. Salary Expectations in IT (India) — 2025 to 2030: Cloud Architects: Rs. 12 – Rs. 24 lakhs per annum AI/ML Engineers: Rs. 8 – Rs. 20 lakhs per annum Cybersecurity Experts: Rs. 7 – Rs. 15 lakhs per annum Blockchain Developers: Rs. 10 – Rs. 25 lakhs per annum Data Engineers: Rs. 8 – Rs. 18 lakhs per annum IoT Engineers: Rs. 7 – Rs. 14 lakhs per annum IT Project Managers: Rs. 15 – Rs. 25 lakhs per annum (depending on experience)

chunks.section: Library Overview, Physical Details and Stack Collection Statistics
chunks.context: Introduction to the MSAJCE Learning Centre (Library) covering area, technology, collection size, and complete stack collection statistics.
chunks.text: Welcome to the Learning Centre — MSAJCE Library is the Heart of every academic Institution. Our institution encourages all students and staff members to make the best use of the library that has been carefully built up since the inception of the college as a cardinal support service of the Institute. The prime objective of our library is to provide the right information to the right readers at the right time in the right manner by bibliographic sources and full text sources in the form of print as well as digital in the disciplines of Science, Engineering, Technology, Humanities, Management, General and etc. The collections are kept in separate places such as the stack section, reference section, and Periodical Section to support the scholarly and informational needs of the Institute Community. The library is housed in a spacious area of 8978 Sq.ft (Ground Floor and 1st Floor), fully computerized with more than 29853 books and 1885 reference volumes stacked for the benefit of staff and students. chunks.section: Institution Membership and Library Rules and Regulations
chunks.context: MSAJCE library institutional memberships (DELNET and J-Gate), and the complete set of library rules, regulations, and fine structure for overdue books.
chunks.text: Institution Membership: 1. Developing Library Network (DELNET) — International Library Network: Avail any book, Xerox or soft copy of any journal article through ILL (Inter Library Loan). URL: https://delnet.in/ 2. J-Gate (Database): More than 50,684 journals are available in the online website. URL: https://jgateplus.com/ Library Rules and Regulations: All Staff and Students are the members of our College Library. All students are eligible to borrow 18 Books. All staff are eligible to borrow Books. Reference books and periodicals will NOT be issued. Library ID is not transferable. Member should notify the Librarian any loss of cards and change of address immediately. The User may reserve a book in circulation. He will be notified as soon as the reserved book is returned. The book will be retained at the circulation desk for one day from the date of return. Books will be issued for a short duration of 30 days only. Fine Structure for Overdue Books: After 30 days — First 7 days (days 31–37): Rs. 1.00 per day per book After 30 days — 8 to 14 days (days 38–44): Rs. 2.00 per day per book After 30 days — 15 days onwards (day 45+): Rs. 5.00 per day per book Additional Rules: Silence should be observed in the Library. Users should leave personal belongings (bags, briefcase, notebooks, files) at the entrance. Loss of books must be reported immediately to the Librarian. If the borrower is unable to replace the lost book, the cost (double amount) will be recovered from the borrower. All books must be returned to the Library in time for physical stock verification. Students are advised to check whether the book contains all pages intact before borrowing. At the time of returning, if any damage or defect is found, the borrower will be held responsible.

chunks.text: National Institutional Ranking Framework (NIRF) — Overview: The National Institutional Ranking Framework (NIRF) is a methodology adopted by the Ministry of Education (MoE), Government of India, to rank institutions of higher education across the country. MSAJCE participates in NIRF rankings annually and submits data across three categories: Engineering, Overall, and Innovation. NIRF 2026 Documents: NIRF 2026 Engineering: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/nift/MSAJCE-ENGG-2026.pdf. NIRF 2026 Overall: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/nift/MSAJCE-OVERALL-2026.pdf. NIRF 2026 Innovation: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/nift/MSAJCE-INNOV-2026.pdf. NIRF 2025 Documents: NIRF 2025 Engineering: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/nift/MSAJCE-ENGG-2025.pdf. chunks.section: Placement Cell Overview — Mission, Objectives & Placement Committee
chunks.context: This chunk describes the overall mission and objectives of the MSAJCE Placement Cell and lists the full placement committee with names and positions.
chunks.text: MSAJCE Placement Cell Overview: In a competitive job market, employers expect graduates to have a range of skills and right experience of the workplace. MSAJCE provides at most care, support and ample opportunities to help students develop requisite skills and expertise and make them employable graduates. Placement Cell Objectives: (1) To provide Employment Opportunities for students in reputed companies. (2) To organize On and Off Campus Recruitment Drives for students. (3) To Train students on employability skills such as Aptitude Skills, Communication Skills and Technical Skills. (4) To conduct Value Added Certification courses to gain domain specific knowledge. (5) To conduct Mock Aptitude, Mock Interview, Mock GD to prepare students to face the interview with confidence. (6) To arrange In-plant training, Internship and Industrial visits. (7) To organize workshops on Recent Technologies by industrial experts which benefits students to understand technological innovations. Besides providing support to entrepreneurial ventures, MSAJCE keeps its employability services up to date with the demands of the job market and aims to ensure that graduates are in the strongest position possible when they leave. Placement Committee: S.No. Name Position: 1 Mr. S.V. Vinodh Placement Officer. 2 Mr. Ajin Sijo John Assistant Placement Officer. 3 Mrs. N. Kavitha Member. 4 Mr. V.A. Babu Charies Earnest Member.

chunks.section: Placement Facilities — Infrastructure for Recruitment Activities
chunks.context: This chunk describes the physical and infrastructure facilities available at MSAJCE for conducting placement activities including written tests, group discussions, interviews and career counselling.
chunks.text: MSAJCE Placement Facilities: (1) Well-furnished air-conditioned rooms for written test, group discussion and face-to-face interview. (2) Well-established Computer labs for online training and examinations. (3) Air-conditioned Auditorium / Seminar Halls / Meeting Halls. (4) Dedicated AC rooms for conducting group discussions. (5) Career counselling is available on campus directing students to choose the best possible stream that meets their requirement in a unique way. The students have mentors who handle their problems and make them focused about their targets.
